metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify AC line 5: `docs/grounding.md` has no existing tiers/T2 section — either (a) restate the AC as 'adds a one-line T2 pointer under the existing grounding oracle description (creating a new T2 subsection)' and call it explicitly in-scope for S1, or (b) defer the `docs/grounding.md` edit to S3 (which already owns that file per the parent epic's file_impact) and remove the AC from S1.
findings.finding: AC line 5 asserts the ADR pointer is added 'from its tiers/T2 section' of docs/grounding.md, but docs/grounding.md contains no 'tiers' heading and no 'T2' reference — the section the AC targets does not exist.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria line 5 ('docs/grounding.md links to the new ADR from its tiers/T2 section')
findings.suggested_fix: Change AC line 5 to: 'A one-line T2 forward-reference is added to `docs/grounding.md` (e.g. under the `refute_absence` section describing the T1/member-binding note), noting that T2 is addressed in the new ADR; the section need not be a full tiers breakdown.' This makes the scope concrete and avoids implying a pre-existing section.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify AC#5's named anchor: either identify the existing `docs/grounding.md` section that should host the ADR pointer (e.g. the `refute_absence` subsection at line 23, where T2 is already mentioned inline at line 37), or explicitly add a new 'T2 semantic resolution' subsection as part of this story's scope.
findings.finding: docs/grounding.md has no 'tiers/T2 section' heading — the named anchor for the ADR pointer does not exist. The plan's AC#5 says the pointer goes 'under the tiers section', but a full read of the file reveals the sections are 'The three query surfaces', 'The consumer integration contract', 'Evidence + fail-open', 'Detectors', '.rebar/ language-extensibility slot', and 'Entry points'. T2 appears only inline at line 37 ('member binding is T2') and line 126, never as a section heading. The implementer has no existing section to attach the pointer to without first creating one.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria #5: 'docs/grounding.md links to the new ADR from its tiers/T2 section'
findings.suggested_fix: Update AC#5 to read: 'docs/grounding.md — a one-line pointer to the new ADR is added in the `refute_absence` subsection (near line 37, where T2 is already mentioned inline as the member-binding escalation target).' This anchors the edit to a real, verifiable location.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile the docs/grounding.md edit ownership between S1 (AC#5 pointer) and S3 (epic 850f file_impact 'S3 T2 docs'): either move the grounding.md pointer to S3 (where the full T2 section is being written) and drop it from S1's AC, or explicitly add docs/grounding.md to S1's file_impact in the parent epic so the split is recorded.
findings.finding: S1 claims ownership of a `docs/grounding.md` edit (AC#5), but the parent epic 850f's file_impact explicitly assigns docs/grounding.md to S3 ('S3 T2 docs'). If S1 adds a T2 pointer and S3 later rewrites the same area to add the full T2 section, the two stories will conflict on the same file.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria #5 (S1 plan) vs. epic 850f file_impact entry: {'path': 'docs/grounding.md', 'reason': 'S3 T2 docs'}

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the AC exactly where in `docs/grounding.md` the S1 one-line ADR pointer goes, given the T2 section is S3's deliverable — e.g., specify a stub `## T2 semantic resolution` heading added by S1 (for S3 to expand), or name the exact existing prose location (e.g., the `refute_absence` section mentioning 'member binding is T2').
findings.finding: The AC requires placing the `docs/grounding.md` pointer 'from its tiers/T2 section', but no such section exists in the current `grounding.md` — the T2 section is S3's responsibility (per the epic's file_impact). An implementer has no specified anchor for the one-line pointer at S1 time, creating a concrete placement ambiguity.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — '`docs/grounding.md` links to the new ADR from its tiers/T2 section'
findings.suggested_fix: Change the AC item to: '`docs/grounding.md` gains a stub `## T2 semantic resolution` subsection (one line: a pointer to the new ADR); S3 expands this stub with the full T2 section.' This makes S1's placement unambiguous and prevents a conflict when S3 lands.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the project-management system (e.g., GitHub Issues via `gh issue create`) and a concrete proving command (e.g., `gh issue view <id> --json body`) for AC6 so an agent can create and verify the sibling epic + relates_to link without stopping to ask.
findings.finding: The plan specifies creation of a sibling epic and a 'relates_to' link but does not state which project-management system (GitHub Issues, Jira, Linear, etc.) or the exact command/UI action to use, leaving an agent without a concrete mechanism to act on or verify AC6.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC6 / What section ('Create a sibling epic for the IaC grounding lane…')
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC6: 'Verified via `gh issue view <EPIC-ID> --json body,projectItems` showing the IaC T0/T1/T2 scope and a cross-reference to epic 850f.' Add to the What section: 'Create using `gh issue create --label epic --title "IaC grounding lane" --body "…"` and link via `gh issue edit <EPIC-ID> --add-label relates-to-850f` or equivalent repo convention.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile the ADR filename: either confirm that 0030 is the next free number and replace '00NN' in the What section with '0030', or change AC 1 to use the same '00NN (next free)' placeholder and instruct the implementer to substitute the actual next number at implementation time.
findings.finding: The What section uses the placeholder 'next free ADR number' (written as '00NN') while AC 1 hard-codes the specific filename '0030-code-grounding-t2-semantic-resolution.md'. If ADR 0030 is already occupied, AC 1 would fail even though the described deliverable (the ADR itself) exists under a different number — sending the implementer in two directions.
findings.location: What section ('Add an ADR at docs/adr/00NN-…') vs. AC 1 ('docs/adr/0030-code-grounding-t2-semantic-resolution.md exists')
findings.suggested_fix: If 0030 is confirmed free: replace '00NN' with '0030' in the What section so both sections agree. If uncertain: change AC 1 to read 'docs/adr/<next-free-number>-code-grounding-t2-semantic-resolution.md exists …' so the AC verifies the content/format rather than the exact filename.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify the heading format against an actual sibling ADR (e.g. `docs/adr/0029-reconciler-bidirectional-status-and-echo.md`) and update AC line 1's parenthetical to list the headings that sibling actually uses, so the format claim is grounded.
findings.finding: AC line 1 asserts the ADR 'follows the repo's ADR format (Status/Date/Context/Decision/Consequences headings, matching a sibling ADR's structure)' but the only sibling ADR read (0029) uses only Status + Context as named headings — no Date or Consequences heading — making the asserted heading set unverifiable against the actual repo convention.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria line 1 ('follows the repo's ADR format (Status/Date/Context/Decision/Consequences headings, matching a sibling ADR's structure)')

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Before committing the ADR, verify the pyright `--outputjson` diagnostic rule-code names (`reportMissingImports`, `reportMissingModuleSource`, `reportAttributeAccessIssue`, `reportUndefinedVariable`) and the JSON output structure against at least one captured real `pyright --outputjson` invocation, and include the verified pyright version and the actual top-level JSON key path in the ADR's Decision section.
findings.finding: The ADR this story produces locks in specific pyright `--outputjson` diagnostic rule-code names (`reportMissingImports`, `reportMissingModuleSource`, `reportAttributeAccessIssue`, `reportUndefinedVariable`) and the JSON output structure as durable architectural decisions, but these are unverified capability assertions: pyright is confirmed absent from the local dev environment, no in-tree `--outputjson` fixture exists, and the pyright JSON output schema has evolved across 1.1.x releases. An ADR that names the wrong codes becomes the authoritative wrong answer for S3.
findings.location: What section — 'Confirm-only mapping' bullet; ADR content specification; AC item 3
findings.suggested_fix: Add a subsection to the ADR's Decision section that reads: 'Verified against pyright X.Y.Z `--outputjson` output: the diagnostics array is at `generalDiagnostics[*]`; the rule code is at `.rule`; import-resolution codes are `reportMissingImports` and `reportMissingModuleSource`; attribute/undefined codes are `reportAttributeAccessIssue` and `reportUndefinedVariable`.' Capture one real fixture file at `tests/unit/grounding/fixtures/pyright_sample_output.json` and reference it from the ADR before the story closes.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
